articles.name: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.title: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.body:
Use this article if your AI agent doesn't respond as expected, escalates too early, or behaves differently across channels or instances. It covers the most common configuration and setup issues, along with the first checks to make before you contact Zendesk Customer Support.

Preliminary AI agent troubleshooting questions

  1. Is the AI agent enabled and assigned to the right channel? see Creating an AI agent to automatically resolve customer issues.
  2. Are relevant articles, knowledge sources, or integrations published and available to your customers? see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Optimizing your knowledge content for generative AI.
  3. Is the correct language activated and supported?
  4. Have you reviewed the conversation logs for errors, escalation, or no response?
    5. Note: When you answer those questions, you know whether you face a configuration issue, a content issue, a channel, or a language issue, or something that needs Zendesk Customer Support.

When you follow the advice above, review common issues below:

The AI agent doesn’t respond to questions

Common causes to a lack of replies from your AI agent

  • The agent isn’t connected to the channel
  • Articles or sources are unpublished or inaccessible
  • The language isn't active
  • A setup or integration step is incomplete

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm the agent is enabled, assigned to the correct channel, and connected to the right knowledge source. For more information, see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents.
  2. Check whether the language is active and supported

AI agent replies when it should hand off

Common causes

  • The fallback logic is too permissive
  • The procedure routes all ambiguous cases through the AI response step
  • Knowledge content is too broad to trigger a clean handoff

To resolve this issue:

  1. Review your escalation strategies and flows
  2. Verify your fallback behavior, and confidence or routing logic
  3. Test with weak-match examples
AI agent escalates too early or shows a technical error

Common causes

  • Inputs are missing or malformed
  • Flow logic doesn’t handle edge cases well
  • Integration errors or parameter mismatches
  • An unhandled condition in the procedure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check your required parameters and fallback handling
  2. Test the integration in the integration builder and check that the request parameters match
  3. Check the HTTP status code
  4. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  5. Verify the conversation logs for differences between successful and failed cases

For more information, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Knowledge sources are missing, incomplete, or inconsistent

Common causes

  • The help center is not active and published
  • Articles are unpublished or their Visible to field does not allow customers and the AI agent to access the content
  • The source was not imported correctly
  • Different instances are on different rollout states. The feature is gated or not yet available everywhere. Search rules are not logical, labels are missing, or incorrect operators are used.

To resolve this issue:

  1. If you use articles from the Zendesk help center, ensure that the help center is active and published
  2. Ensure that article settings are published and the Placement > Visible to field is set to Everyone
  3. Confirm the knowledge sources were added in the right place, imported successfully, and is accessible to the AI agent. Verify imported knowledge sources for AI agents.
  4. Verify if the crawl failed because it timed out, is missing pages, or the crawl includes the right pages but the wrong content. see Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents and Using a web crawler to index external content.
  5. Compare configuration search rules between instances. for AI agents Advanced only
  6. Check for restricted articles and where the end user does not have access
AI agent behaves differently by language

Common causes to multiple responses for each language

  • The language hasn’t been activated
  • The agent is falling back because it can’t detect or serve the target language
  • The content exists, but not in the expected language

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm that the target language is activated
  2. Language detection is configured
  3. Content exists in the correct language
AI agent integration is failing

Common causes to a failed app integration with AI agents

  • Invalid credentials
  • Unsupported payload format
  • Endpoint or mapping errors
  • External system timeout or failure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check the connection
  2. Verify the authentication and conversation logs
  3. Test the integration in the integration builder
  4. Check that the request parameters match and the HTTP status code
  5. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  6. Test the integration in the builder if available

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Common causes to a failed CRM connectivity

  • The bot doesn’t join conversations
  • Escalations to agents fail
  • Tagging errors occur during CRM-based flow handling

To resolve this issue:

  1. Verify whether the bot joins conversations
  2. Ensure that agent escalations and human hand offs succeed
  3. Verify if tags are applied correctly

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents.

When to contact Zendesk Customer Support about AI agents

Contact Zendesk Customer Support if:

  • The issue persists after you carefully verify your configuration against this article
  • The behavior is inconsistent across instances
  • You can replicate the issue
  • Logs show an error you can't resolve
  • The issue appears to be a product bug or backend provisioning problem

If you need help to submit a request, see How to contact Customer Support.

Before you contact Zendesk Customer Support

Gather the following details to assist with our investigation:

  • Your Zendesk subdomain or URL
  • The AI agent name
  • Channel
  • Conversation or ticket ID
  • Time of the issue
  • Steps to reproduce
  • Screenshots or screen recording
  • Relevant conversation logs
  • Any related integration details

Additional articles

  • Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents
  • Configuring escalation strategies and flows for AI agents
  • Configuring language detection for AI agents
  • Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents
  • Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents (AI agents - Advanced only)

articles.name: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.title: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.body:
This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

Learn how to accept or decline Contact Center calls, set the right agent status, and use call details before answering. If you can’t take a call, decline it to send it to the next available agent and reduce customer wait time. You also need a supported browser and notifications turned on to get incoming call alerts.

This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

You must use a supported browser and, if you want your operating system to notify you of incoming calls, configure your browser for notifications.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Setting your status for calls
  • Accepting calls
  • Declining calls
Related articles:
  • Understanding how Contact Center calls become tickets

Setting your status for calls

To receive calls, you must set your status to a Zendesk status that's mapped to Available in Amazon Connect. The standard Zendesk status Transfer only isn't supported for Contact Center calls. For information about the standard Zendesk statuses and how to set your status, see Setting your agent status.
Tip: If you're an agent and don't know if a given status maps to Available in Connect, ask your admin.

Accepting calls

To accept a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details, to view any associated information.

    The information provided comes from the contact flow and its contact attributes.
  2. Click Accept.

    When you accept the call, depending on your settings, Zendesk might begin recording the call and create a ticket. For details, see how calls become tickets. A counter shows the duration of the call.

    You can still show or hide the details associated with the call at this point.

  3. Click the close icon (X) to close the call notification and proceed with the call.

Declining calls

Calls are automatically routed to the first available agent. If that agent doesn't answer after a configurable amount of time, it then routes the call to the next available agent.

Don't wait for the allocated time to run out and the call goes to the next available agent. Decline the call to send it straight to the next available agent so customers wait less time.

To decline a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details, to view any associated contact attributes.
  2. Click Decline to reject the call and put it back in the queue.

articles.name: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.title: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.body:
When working with messaging conversations, there are two different ways to use tags:

  1. Client-side messaging metadata, set by the Web Widget or SDKs
  2. Tags added in an AI agent flow, using Sunshine Conversations CRM actions such as Add conversation tags

These two methods can look similar because both can affect the context of a conversation and the ticket created from it. However, they are designed for different use cases and behave differently over time.

This article explains how each option works, when to use it, and what to consider when deciding which one is best for your workflow.

This workflow includes the following sections:

  • Understanding the difference
  • Tagging options for messaging conversations
  • Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations
  • When to use each tagging method
  • Additional resources

Understanding the difference

Client-side messaging metadata

Client-side messaging metadata is set from the frontend, such as:

  • the Web Widget loaded on a website
  • an SDK implementation

With this method, your developer sends metadata from the client into the messaging conversation. This can include conversation fields and conversation tags.

For tags, the Web Widget or SDK sends values using the messaging metadata APIs. In the conversation metadata, this appears as zen:ticket:tags.

This method is useful when the information is already available in the client, such as:

  • the product page the customer is viewing
  • an order number already displayed in the app
  • the customer’s current cart state
  • a known context that should be passed into the ticket

Adding tags to the conversation via a CRM action

This represents the CRM action in AI Advanced Agents that can be applied at different levels (see Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents).

This method is useful when the tag should be determined by the logic of the AI agent conversation itself, for example:

  • the user went through a specific path in a Dialogue or Procedure
  • the bot identified a use case during the conversation
  • the bot collected values that should be reflected in the conversation or downstream ticket

Although both methods use tags, they are not the same mechanism.

Tagging options for messaging conversations

Option 1: Using client-side messaging metadata

If you want to pass tags from the Web Widget or from a mobile SDK, use the client-side messaging metadata APIs.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag value is known when the conversation begins
  • the tag comes from the page or app context
  • you want to pass context into the conversation before a ticket is created
  • you want to manage tag values from the frontend

Examples include:

  • tagging a conversation based on the page category
  • tagging a conversation with a product family
  • tagging a conversation based on the customer’s logged-in experience

Benefits of client-side metadata tags

Client-side metadata tags are a good fit when the tag is tied to the customer’s current session context in the widget or app.

Benefits include:

  • The data can be set automatically without asking the customer to provide it
  • The tags can be set as soon as the page or app state is known
  • The data can be cleared using the messaging metadata APIs when the frontend context changes
  • This approach is well suited to information that is only relevant to the current client-side experience

Option 2: Using tags in an AI agent flow

If you want the bot to decide when tags should be added during a conversation, use the Add conversation tags CRM action in your AI agent flow.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag depends on what happens during the bot flow
  • the tag should be added after the user answers a question
  • the tag reflects the conversation path or intent
  • the tag is part of your AI agent logic rather than your frontend implementation

Examples include:

  • adding a tag after the customer selects a support topic
  • adding a tag when the AI agent identifies a billing or return scenario
  • adding a tag to influence downstream routing or reporting

Benefits of AI agent flow tags

Using Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow is a good fit when the tag should be controlled by the bot itself.

Benefits include:

  • Tags can be added based on conversation logic
  • Tags can use values collected during the flow
  • Tags can be applied consistently across channels where the AI agent is used
  • Tags can help downstream routing, reporting, or field selection logic

Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations

When using AI agent flow tagging with Sunshine Conversations, it is important to understand that information stored on a Sunshine Conversations conversation persists on that conversation.

In Sunshine Conversations, a conversation is a persistent object. Metadata and tags added to that conversation remain associated with it until they are changed or overwritten.

Because of this, Add conversation tags should be used with a clear understanding of how long the tag should remain attached to the conversation.

This leads to two valid implementation choices.

Choice 1: The tag should persist on the conversation

In some workflows, persistent tagging is the correct behavior.

Use this option when the tag is meant to remain associated with the conversation as part of the intended workflow, such as:

  • identifying the overall type of conversation
  • preserving a long-lived classification for future use
  • supporting downstream handling across the same Sunshine Conversations conversation

Choice 2: The tag is only valid for the current session

In other workflows, the tag is only relevant to the current session or a specific moment in the flow.

In this case, you should take steps to prevent the tag from carrying over unintentionally in the same Sunshine Conversations conversation.

A practical way to do this is to use the CRM action Overwrite conversation tags to clear the existing tags where needed.

For example, you can:

  • clear the conversation tags at the beginning of the flow
  • clear them at a specific point before applying new session-specific tags
  • clear them whenever you want to avoid carrying over metadata from an earlier interaction

If needed, an empty value can be submitted using a space character (" ") to effectively clear the existing conversation tags in the flow.

This approach is useful when tags are intended to be session-scoped rather than conversation-scoped.

When to use each tagging method

Client-side messaging metadata

Choose client-side messaging metadata when:

  • the tag value is known in the Web Widget or SDK
  • the information comes from the page or app context
  • the tag should be managed from the frontend
  • the context is tied to the current widget or app experience

Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow

Choose Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow when:

  • the bot should decide when to add the tag
  • the tag depends on user responses or flow logic
  • the tag is part of the AI agent workflow
  • persistent conversation-level tagging is acceptable or intended

If you use Add conversation tags for information that is only valid for the current session, plan how to clear or overwrite those tags so they do not carry over unexpectedly.

The most important distinction is behavior over time:

  • Client-side metadata is designed around frontend-managed context
  • Sunshine Conversations conversation tags persist on the conversation until they are changed or overwritten

Choosing the right method depends on whether the tag should reflect client context or bot flow logic, and whether the tag should be temporary or persistent.

Additional resources

If you would like to learn more, see:

  • Using messaging metadata with the Zendesk Web Widget and SDKs
  • About configured actions for AI agents
  • Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents
  • Available CRM actions for AI agents and Sunshine Conversations
  • Sunshine Conversations key concepts

articles.name: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.title: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.body:
Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?

Explore reports auto assist activity based on agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, not every time auto assist triggers or generates content.

A ticket is included in the current auto assist reporting only when an agent interacts with a suggestion, for example, when an agent performs one of these tasks:

  • Accepts it
  • Edits it
  • Dismisses it

If auto assist generates a suggestion but the agent does not interact with it, that activity is not included in the available Copilot auto assist dataset.

This also means some tickets do not appear in reporting even though auto assist was active on the ticket, for example:

  • A suggestion was still being generated and auto assist was turned off before the suggestion was available
  • A suggestion was generated, but it did not contain any actionable content for the agent to interact with
  • A suggestion was generated, but the agent did not use or respond to it

Because of this, auto assist metrics in Explore can be lower than counts from ticket searches or other reports with the agent_copilot_enabled ticket tag. Those sources can show where auto assist is turned on, triggered, or generated a suggestion, while Explore reflects only suggestions that result in recorded agent interaction.

The current auto assist reporting is a measure of agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, rather than a complete count of every auto assist trigger, generated suggestion, or ticket where a procedure applies.

articles.name: Announcing view conditions for requester email address
articles.title: Announcing view conditions for requester email address
articles.body:
We’re excited to announce a new condition to Zendesk views under requester fields, that lets you filter tickets by the requester’s email address.

This article includes the following sections:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Previously, views did not support filtering directly by requester email. With this update, you can now create views using the Requester’s email address with the following operators:

  • Is
  • Is not

To help prevent an invalid configuration, Zendesk will validate the email address format before a view can be saved.

This makes it easier to create precise views that target tickets based on requester email criteria.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Customers need a more flexible and accurate way to organize and monitor tickets in views. Filtering by requester email address helps teams quickly surface tickets from specific users, exclude certain requesters, and better manage workflows.

This enhancement improves ticket routing and visibility while keeping existing views functionality unchanged.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. This feature will be rolled out to all accounts over the next week. Once available in your account, you'll automatically see the updated verification icons in your Customer List

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Announcing new action builder connectors
articles.title: Announcing new action builder connectors
articles.body:
We're excited to announce five new connectors for action flows, bringing essential business automation capabilities to your Zendesk workflows: HubSpot, Chargebee, Stripe, Calendly, and GitHub.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What's changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What's changing?

New connectors bring customer lifecycle automation and cross-team collaboration directly into Zendesk action flows: 

  • With HubSpot, teams can create and update contacts, companies, deals, and tickets to keep CRM records in sync. 
  • Chargebee supports subscription actions like provisioning, billing changes, pauses, cancellations, and credit notes. 
  • Stripe enables refunds, invoice creation, customer payment searches, and other billing tasks without leaving Zendesk. 
  • Calendly simplifies scheduling with booking links, invitations, and event searches.
  • GitHub helps support teams escalate bugs and feature requests, create and update pull requests, and track engineering progress—all from one place.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Support teams often work across CRM, billing, scheduling, and engineering tools to manage customer requests, but manually updating records, handling billing changes, coordinating meetings, and escalating bugs can slow resolution and create gaps in context. 

These new connectors bring customer lifecycle automation, scheduling, and engineering collaboration directly into action flows, enabling teams to sync CRM data, manage subscriptions and payments, automate bookings, and route product feedback or issues without leaving Zendesk.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. These connectors are being rolled out to all accounts with access to action flows. 

To learn more about each connector and how to connect them to action builder, see:

  • Using Calendly actions in action flows
  • Using Chargebee actions in action flows
  • Using HubSpot actions in action flows
  • Using Stripe actions in action flows
  • Using GitHub actions in action flows

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.title: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When you try adding an email address to your Support addresses, or bulk update users with a comma-separated values (CSV) file, you receive an error or an email with a link to download an Excel file: Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email.

Resolving the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error

This error means you are trying to use an email address already used by another user on your account. That account may have been created by an email forwarded to your Support address.

To resolve the This email is already taken error:

  1. First, verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support with the email address from the error and confirm which user account already is associated with that email address
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change
  4. A final option if you have multiple users is to merge your users

For more information, see this article: Bulk importing users.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I receive the error "This email is already taken. Try another email"? What does the error "Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email" mean? Why can I not add an email address or update users?

This error means another user or customer account on your Zendesk Support help desk already uses that email address. An email forwarded to your Support address sometimes creates that duplicate account.

How do I resolve the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error? How do I fix the user couldn't be updated error when bulk updating users or adding a Support address? How can I repair duplicate email address conflicts?

  1. Verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it.
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support or your ticketing system with the email address from the error and confirm which user or customer account already is associated with that email address.
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change.
  4. Merge your users or team members if you have multiple users.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.title: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.body:
Summary

On June 19, 2026, between 14:19 UTC and 15:01 UTC, AI Agents customers across all pods experienced service delays and errors when using AI features such as composing and enhancing messages, due to an issue affecting the Open AI service in the GCP EU region.

Timeline

June 19, 2026 16: UTC | June 19, 2026 9:30 AM PT

We are aware of an issue that affected AI Agents and Zendesk Support AI features between 14:09 and 15:01 UTC on June 19. The incident was caused by a service degradation with our vendor, OpenAI, resulting in slow responses or intermittent errors for some customers. This issue has been fully resolved, and all services are operating normally. We apologize for the inconvenience caused.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an upstream service disruption in the EU region that impacted the flow of data through our system, resulting in cascading failures that affected AI agent performance.

Resolution

The issue was resolved by monitoring until the external service stabilized and recovered, after which related systems resumed normal operation without the need for manual intervention.

Remediation Items

  1. Explore options for automatic failover with the API provider to minimize service disruptions.
  2. Investigate why the on-call team was not contacted by phone during this incident and improve notification processes.
  3. Replace the global fallback setting with service-specific fallback configurations for better reliability.
  4. Confirm whether the fallback option for the Azure OpenAI service is properly configured.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Scheduled Maintenance -  July 1, 2026 | All Pods - Copilot Features
articles.title: Scheduled Maintenance -  July 1, 2026 | All Pods - Copilot Features
articles.body:
Zendesk will perform critical maintenance impacting Copilot features for customers on all Pods on July 1, 2026. Maintenance will occur from 10am UTC to 8pm UTC, with the maintenance for each account lasting up to 15 minutes. We are upgrading our database for improved performance and ongoing reliability.

Affected products: Copilot features

Important:  Only customers using Copilot features will be impacted.

Customers Affected: Zendesk customers on all Pods will experience a brief service disruption of up to 15 minutes at some time within the 10am UTC to 8pm UTC maintenance window.

 

Expected behavior: The database upgrade may cause noticeable delays in features such as Auto Assist, Intelligent Triage, and other Copilot features.

What do customers have to do? No action is required. Features will return to normal service following the maintenance.

Why we're doing this: We are increasing database size to provide additional performance and ensure stability of our systems going forward.

articles.name: Announcing Amazon S3 as an external content source for use across knowledge experiences
articles.title: Announcing Amazon S3 as an external content source for use across knowledge experiences
articles.body:
We’re excited to announce the general availability of Amazon S3 as a knowledge source. You can now connect Amazon S3 to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

You can now connect Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used. This expands the content sources available, enabling access to your existing Amazon S3 content across your knowledge experiences.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

We’re making it easier to reuse existing knowledge from different sources your organization already uses and leverage it within Zendesk. So now, your AI agents, search, and generative search can tap into your Amazon S3 knowledge.

What do I need to do?

You can create an Amazon S3 connection on any Zendesk Suite or Knowledge plan. For more information, see the following resources:

  • Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source
  • Connecting knowledge sources to power generative replies in agents

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-06-19
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-06-19
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Voice
  • App Marketplace
  • Products with no updates this week

Voice

New:

  • Admins can now configure how many times an IVR greeting repeats (from one to three) before routing to the default option. See Routing incoming calls with IVR.

Fixed:

  • Improvements to the speaker diarization model used to label speakers as "Agent" and "Customer" in post-call transcripts. 

App Marketplace

New:
  • XCHK Verification (Support)
    • XCHK Verification provides real-time identity verification for remote interactions. XCHK Verification lets Zendesk agents launch a live video verification session directly from a ticket sidebar. The app verifies the requester's real-time location against their expected location and checks device authenticity, including detection of browser language/timezone spoofing and VPN/proxy use. After the session, PASS or FAIL results are posted back to the Zendesk ticket as an internal note with tags, and can trigger Zendesk automations. When Zendesk OAuth is connected in XCHK, results sync automatically back to the ticket.
  • Quick Charts (Support) (paid)
    • Quick Charts puts live ticket visualizations one click away. Open the top bar popover, pick a view, and see your queue broken down by status, priority, assignee workload, channel, and ticket type - all without leaving the agent workspace.
  • Macro Hero (Support)
    • Macro Hero is the missing macro management tool for Zendesk admins and CX teams. If you have hundreds (or thousands) of macros, you know how painful it is to keep them organized, up to date, and consistent. Macro Hero brings powerful bulk operations, inline editing, and usage analytics — all in a clean, intuitive interface that lives right in your Zendesk navigation bar.
  • Uniti AI (Support) (paid)
    • Uniti AI handles your customer conversations across voice, SMS, WhatsApp, email, and web chat. This integration captures every one of them as a structured Zendesk ticket — with the full transcript, an AI-generated summary, intent tags, and custom field data — so your support, sales, and marketing teams see the full picture in real time.
  • LeadDesk Talk v2 (Support)
    • LeadDesk Talk v2 helps your agents manage calls and SMS directly in Zendesk. Call and SMS activity is matched to tickets under a customer profile, so you can track the entire ticket history in Zendesk. Make and receive support calls directly in Zendesk.  Send SMS follow-ups with additional support messages.  Calls open new tickets, and messages are recorded as notes.
  • toern returns (Support)
    • toern optimizes returns and exchange requests through intelligent, automated workflows. The solution integrates seamlessly into existing shop and logistics systems, transforming the return process into a positive customer experience. By leveraging AI, toern relieves the burden on support teams and provides valuable data to optimize your business operations. Give full visibility into all return processes directly within Zendesk Support tickets.
  • Data Manager (Support) (paid)
    • Data Manager is an audit and visibility tool for Zendesk admins.  Data Manager gives you a fast, sortable spreadsheet view of your Users, Organizations, and Business Rules — built right into the agent interface.
  • ProductBridge (Support)
    • ProductBridge helps product teams capture, organize, and prioritize customer feedback from Zendesk tickets. The sidebar app lets support agents search existing feedback posts, create new ones, upvote on behalf of customers, and link tickets to posts — all without leaving Zendesk. AI-powered detection surfaces product feedback automatically, and smart deduplication keeps things tidy across all connected sources (Zendesk, Slack, Intercom, Jira, Linear, and more).
  • Onetime Secret (Support)
    • Onetime Secret lets you create encrypted, self-destructing links to share passwords, credentials, and other sensitive data with customers directly from your ticket workspace. Secrets are encrypted at rest, viewable exactly once, then permanently deleted. Supports all Onetime Secret regions (US, EU, CA, UK, NZ) and custom domains for branded sharing links.
  • Super Views (Support) (paid)
    • Super Views replaces native Zendesk ticket views with a faster, richer way to see and triage your tickets. Super Views shows up to 1000 tickets per view with multi-column sorting, color coding, and instant CSV export - all inside the agent workspace.

Products with no updates this week

  • Admin Center
  • AI agents
  • Support
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • Copilot
  • Knowledge
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Understanding how Contact Center calls become tickets
articles.title: Understanding how Contact Center calls become tickets
articles.body:
When an agent answers or makes a call through the console, Zendesk creates a ticket. The ticket is associated with the calling number in either an existing end-user profile or a new profile that uses the calling number as the direct line. This article describes the circumstances where Zendesk creates and doesn’t create new tickets on calls and how profiles are linked to tickets.

Summary: ◀▼

When you answer or make calls through the console, a ticket is created and linked to the caller's profile. Inbound calls or voicemails generate new tickets unless linked to an open ticket ID. Outbound calls create tickets unless initiated from the ticket composer. Tickets include call details and optionally recordings or transcripts. You can merge new tickets with existing ones if the call relates to an ongoing issue.

When an agent answers or makes a call through the console, Zendesk creates a ticket. The ticket is associated with the calling number in either an existing end-user profile or a new profile that uses the calling number as the direct line. This article describes the circumstances where Zendesk creates and doesn’t create new tickets on calls and how profiles are linked to tickets.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Understanding how calls become tickets
  • Merging tickets when a call is about an existing ticket

Understanding how calls become tickets

On inbound calls, Zendesk creates a new ticket when an agent answers a call or when an end user leaves a voicemail, unless the ticket_id contact attribute is set in the contact flow. When Zendesk receives the ticket_id of an open ticket, the call or voicemail is associated with that ticket. If the ticket_id corresponds to a ticket that doesn't exist or that is closed, then a new ticket is created.

On outbound calls, Zendesk creates a ticket unless the agent initiates the call from the channel switcher in the ticket composer. If the agent initiates a call from the console or from an end-user profile, Zendesk creates a new ticket. For more details, see Making outbound calls in Contact Center.

When a ticket is created, it contains details about the call or voicemail including:

  • Overview
    • Direction: inbound or outbound.
    • From: caller phone number.
    • To: destination phone number.
    • Date: weekday, month and day of month.
  • Caller information
    • Type: phone, mobile, or SIP.
    • Queue name: name of the queue in Amazon Connect.
  • Post call
    • (Optional) Recording consent: whether the caller opted in or out to the recording of the call.

Optionally, depending on the configuration, it can also contain:

  • A recording of the call, which may be redacted.
  • A transcript of the call, which may be redacted.
  • With the Copilot add-on, a summary of the call transcript.

Merging tickets when a call is about an existing ticket

Sometimes, callers might contact you about an issue already covered in an existing ticket. To handle this situation, complete the call and add comments as needed to the new ticket. You can then merge the new ticket with the existing ticket.

The phone number shown at the top of a ticket is the phone number that caused the ticket to be created. When you merge two tickets that both have voice comments, Zendesk shows the number associated with the ticket you merge into. For details, see Merging tickets.

articles.name: What are the WhatsApp moderation decisions in Zendesk?
articles.title: What are the WhatsApp moderation decisions in Zendesk?
articles.body:
What are the WhatsApp moderation decisions in Zendesk?

When you connect your WhatsApp number in Zendesk, Zendesk provides the integration, but WhatsApp (Meta) remains the decision-maker for certain moderation-related actions.

That means Zendesk does not have control over:

  • Display name approval
  • Template categorization
  • Any other WhatsApp moderation or review decisions

If a display name, template, or account-related action is rejected, submit the appeal directly through WhatsApp or Meta business tools. Zendesk cannot override or appeal those decisions on your behalf.

This article contains the following sections:

  • How to manage templates and phone numbers in WhatsApp Business Manager
  • How to appeal other account activity decisions

How to manage templates and phone numbers in WhatsApp Business Manager

To manage your templates and phone numbers:

  1. Sign in to your Meta account and access your company portfolio
  2. Select the WhatsApp Business account you want to manage
  3. Click WhatsApp Manager

How to appeal other account activity decisions

For any other appeal related to your WhatsApp business account activity, go to:

https://business.facebook.com/business-support-home/<businessID>/<assetID>/

To replace:

  • <businessID> with your Business ID
  • <assetID> with the Asset ID for your template or account

You can find these values in the URL of your WhatsApp Business Manager account.

Important: While Zendesk makes it possible to connect and use WhatsApp in the app, WhatsApp or Meta handles all moderation, approval, and appeal decisions. Make any request to review or reverse those decisions through the appropriate WhatsApp Business or Meta Business interface.

articles.name: Announcing configurable IVR greeting repeat count for Zendesk Voice
articles.title: Announcing configurable IVR greeting repeat count for Zendesk Voice
articles.body:
We’re updating Zendesk Voice (Talk) multi-level interactive voice response (IVR) to give admins more control over the caller experience and to modernize the settings interface.

This announcement answers the following questions:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Previously, the IVR greeting repeated three times before the call fell back to the default option. This behavior was hardcoded, which limited flexibility for customers seeking a faster routing flow.

With this update, admins can now configure how many times the IVR greeting repeats, from one to three, before routing to the default option.

To preserve the current experience and avoid any disruptions, the default remains at three.

We’ve also refreshed the IVR settings page to match Zendesk’s modern look and feel for a cleaner, more consistent experience.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

This update gives admins more flexibility to tune IVR flows to their needs. A quicker fallback to the default option will help callers reach the right agent or department faster. If preferred, the existing three-repeat behavior will continue to work as expected.

The updated IVR settings page aligns with Zendesk's latest platform design standards.

What do I need to do?

  • No action is required if you want to keep the current behavior.
  • If you want callers to reach the default option sooner, update the repeat count in your IVR settings. For more information, see Routing incoming calls with IVR.

If you have feedback or questions about this announcement, visit our community forum, where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Service Incident - May 29, 2026 - Support | All Pods - 401 blank page errors in Salesforce Apps
articles.title: Service Incident - May 29, 2026 - Support | All Pods - 401 blank page errors in Salesforce Apps
articles.body:
Summary

Between May 29, 2026, 07:41 UTC and June 1, 2026, 01:54 UTC, customers using Salesforce Zendesk Ticket Viewer and Canvas app across multiple pods experienced blank pages and access errors that prevented viewing Zendesk tickets. Additionally, some customers faced disruptions in synchronizing data between Salesforce and Zendesk.

Timeline

May 29, 2026 12:31 UTC | May 29, 2026 5:31 AM PT

We are aware of 4XX blank page errors occurring on Salesforce apps. While our engineers actively investigate the issue, we recommend the following steps to potentially resolve it: navigate to the Admin Center, select Integrations, disconnect the Salesforce app via the menu, and then reconnect your Salesforce instance. Please provide feedback on whether these steps resolve the issue, and contact us if the problem persists.

May 29, 2026 15:18 UTC | May 29, 2026 8:18 AM PT

Thanks for reporting this. It turns out that Zendesk developers recently had to make a change to the Oauth Token renewal portion of our integration with Salesforce. This change may have impacted the token that would've been used by your Salesforce integration. Could you please try taking the following steps to see if it helps to resolve the issue:

Open a new INCOGNITO browser window - this is to help avoid issues that can arise due to browser caching.

  1. Within that incognito window, navigate to Admin Center > Apps and Integrations > Salesforce
  2. Click into your Salesforce connection that's failing
  3. Click the Actions drop-down and then Disconnect button in the top-right corner of the page:
  4. You can immediately re-add the connection (still within that Incognito window) - all your previous settings will be saved, but you'll need to re-enable the features you want to use by checking the Enable checkbox on those feature pages.
  5. When reconnecting, please be sure to use a user that has the required profile permissions.

May 29, 2026 17:19 UTC | May 29, 2026 10:19 AM PT

We have another update regarding the issues impacting the Zendesk Salesforce integration. We apologize for the continued inconvenience, but we are now investigating an issue that is impacting the Salesforce ticket viewer indicating users do not have permission to view Zendesk tickets.
 
We will provide an update as soon as one is available. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated.

May 29, 2026 18:37 UTC | May 29, 2026 11:37 AM PT

Just an update, our engineers are still actively investigating the issue preventing some user's access to the Salesforce Zendesk Ticket Viewer, accessed within Salesforce.
 
If you are still experiencing issues accessing the Zendesk Salesforce Sidebar APP as seen in Zendesk Support, please proceed with reconnecting your Salesforce integration and ensure that you grant access to all users while doing so.
 
We will provide additional updates and/or instructions to restore full access to the integration platform as soon as possible.

May 29, 2026 21:07 UTC | May 29, 2026 2:07 PM PT

We are continuing our investigation and working with Salesforce to identify the root cause and resolve the issue impacting access to the integration. Thank you for your understanding and patience.

May 30, 2026 00:26 UTC | May 29, 2026 5:26 PM PT

We’re continuing to work closely with Salesforce on this issue and have an active support case open with them, including diagnostic information we’ve gathered. In parallel, we are testing an alternative workaround/fix to restore ticket viewer access for impacted users, and we’ll share another update as soon as it becomes available.
Thank you for your patience as we continue to work to resolve this.

May 30, 2026 3:06 UTC | May 29, 2026 8:06 PM PT

We are glad to update you that the workaround fix has been deployed and enabled for your account. Please validate in your Salesforce integration that users can access the Zendesk ticket viewer again, and let us know your feedback.
 
If you are still seeing the issue after the workaround has been enabled for your account, please disconnect and reconnect the Zendesk–Salesforce integration (this refreshes the connection state/tokens), then test again. 
If it continues to fail after reconnecting, please reply with the affected Salesforce user(s), a timestamp of the most recent failure, and a screenshot of the error so we can investigate further.
 
Thank you for your patience as we continue to work to resolve this.

June 2, 2026 6:30 UTC | June 1, 2026 11:30 PM PT

We continue to observe recovery after the deployment of the fix across affected customers, and we are monitoring closely.
 
If you are still experiencing issues, please disconnect and reconnect the Zendesk–Salesforce integration using the steps in this guide:
https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408834115738-Setting-up-ticket-view-in-Salesforce
Please ensure that under OAuth Policies, Permitted Users are set to: Admin approved users are pre-authorized, then click Save. This is a required setting.
Users do not need to re-authenticate with OAuth each time.
 
We also recommend reviewing the Salesforce integration resources guide here and following the setup instructions carefully:
https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408827957274-Salesforce-integration-resources
 
After reconnecting and reviewing the guides, kindly please test again.
Thank you for your patience.

June 4, 2026 1:21 UTC | June 3, 2026 6:21 PM PT

We would like to inform you that the issue was not caused by Zendesk or by customer actions. The issue appears to be on the Salesforce side, and we are actively coordinating with them to have it resolved as soon as possible.
 
We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this has caused and greatly appreciate your understanding.

June 8, 2026 6:29 UTC | June 7, 2026 11:29 PM PT

The Salesforce-side product setting bug has been disabled, fully restoring integration services. However, because the issue invalidated existing tokens, customers who are still experiencing problems with their Salesforce integration — such as Ticket Viewer issues or account sync errors that started on Friday, May 29, 2026 — will need to complete a one-time disconnect and reconnect of the integration.
This step is required to refresh the connection and generate new valid tokens. Please carefully review the setup guides below before reconnecting:
 

  • https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408827957274-Salesforce-integration-resources
  • https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408834115738-Setting-up-ticket-view-in-Salesforce


Key steps
 

  1. Confirm the Connected App setup forSalesforce Integration for Zendesk matches our documentation, including:
    • OAuth policy set toAdmin approved users are pre-authorized
    • Relevant profiles added underManage Profiles
  2. Revoke OAuth tokens for the Connected App in Salesforce
  3. Note any active integration features for later re-enabling
  4. Disconnect the integration in Zendesk Admin Center
  5. Reconnect the integration using an admin with appropriate permissions
  6. Re-enable the required features from step 3
  7. Test to confirm all features — especially Ticket View — work for all relevant users


If you continue to experience issues after completing these steps, or if you need guidance on the process, please let us know.

June 11, 2026 00:48 UTC | June 10, 2026 5:48 PM PT

The issue affecting access to the Salesforce Zendesk Ticket Viewer within Salesforce has now been resolved.

Root Cause Analysis

The incident was caused by a configuration error on our partner provider's side related to security settings that invalidated login tokens, resulting in widespread access errors and missing content within the provider's ticket view app.

Resolution

To fix the problem, a temporary solution was activated to immediately restore Ticket View access. Our team worked closely with the partner provider to disable the setting that was causing login token problems, confirmed recovery, removed the temporary fix, and supported affected customers with a one-time disconnect and reconnect to refresh tokens.

Remediation Items

  1. Review alert settings with the service reliability team to understand why the issue was not flagged promptly.
  2. Update the internal guidance pages related to the partner/provider integration.
  3. Review and improve partner/provider integration setup documentation to ensure accuracy.
  4. Enhance monitoring for the partner/provider features that were impacted.
  5. Create a clear, updated support and escalation guide for handling partner/provider-related production incidents.
  6. Continue working closely with the partner/provider to identify the root cause and develop a permanent fix.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Announcing access log AI descriptions for easier review
articles.title: Announcing access log AI descriptions for easier review
articles.body:
Zendesk is adding AI-generated descriptions for individual access log entries to make complex event data easier to interpret at a glance. 

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What's changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What's changing?

This update is a continuation of recent access log enhancements and responds to customer needs for faster, clearer investigations. Zendesk is introducing an AI-powered, on-demand, detailed log description for individual access log events to help less-technical users quickly understand what happened, why it may matter, and what to check next.

This enhancement is designed to make access logs easier to use for security, compliance, and fraud investigations without requiring admins to manually interpret complex log data.

Updates include:

  • An AI-generated event description: Creates a structured natural-language explanation for a single log event.
  • Simplified language: Translates technical log data into easier-to-understand wording.
  • Investigation guidance: Highlights why an event may be relevant and suggests what to review next.
  • Better usability for non-technical users: Helps make access logs more approachable and actionable.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Zendesk is improving access logs to make investigations faster and easier for a broader range of users, including those who are less technical. Customers often need to understand individual events quickly, but raw log data can be difficult to interpret.

By adding AI-powered descriptions, Zendesk helps customers:

  • Understand access events more easily
  • Reduce manual effort during investigations
  • Improve security and compliance workflows
  • Act on log data with more confidence

What do I need to do?

No action is required. This enhancement will be activated automatically on the rollout date, provided you have both access logs enabled and admin copilot conversational assistance turned on.

For more information, see Using the access log to monitor agent activity.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Making outbound calls in Contact Center
articles.title: Making outbound calls in Contact Center
articles.body:
You can make outbound calls inContact Centerregardless of yourstatus, be it online, offline, away, transfers only, or any custom status.

Summary: ◀▼

You can make outbound calls regardless of your status, using your profile settings to choose between browser or phone calls. Calls can be made from the call console, existing tickets, or user profiles, with call details added to new or existing tickets. Your routing profile controls the outbound line, and admins can configure additional calling regions if needed.

You can make outbound calls in Contact Center regardless of your status, be it online, offline, away, transfers only, or any custom status.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Considerations for making outbound calls
  • Making an outbound call from the call console
  • Making an outbound call from an existing ticket
  • Making an outbound call from a user profile

Considerations for making outbound calls

When you make an outbound call:

  • Your profile settings determine whether the phone call occurs in the browser or your phone. For more information, see Configuring voice preferences in Contact Center. If you configure your phone, then Zendesk makes a call to your phone before connecting you to the customer.

  • Your Amazon Connect routing profile determines the outbound line. If you want to make an outbound call and need to change the outbound line, contact your admin to set your routing profile accordingly.

  • By default, Amazon Connect supports outbound calling only to specific regions. If needed, ask your admin to configure additional regions.

  • If you make an outbound call without attaching it to an existing ticket, you can always merge the new ticket from the call with an existing ticket. For more information, see Merging tickets.

Making an outbound call from the call console

When you make a call from the call console, the call generates a new ticket.

To make an outbound call from the call console

  1. In Support, click the call console icon () in the top bar.

  2. In the call console, enter the phone number you want to call using the dial pad or your keyboard.

    The outbound line is determined by your routing profile in Amazon Connect.

  3. Click Call when you are ready to initiate the phone call.

    If you're using your own phone, Zendesk will call your number and then dial the recipient after you pick up.

Making an outbound call from an existing ticket

When you make a call from a ticket, by default, the call details are added to that ticket. You can initiate an outbound call from any ticket that isn’t closed, even if it wasn’t created by an incoming call.

To make an outbound call from an existing ticket

  1. In Support, open the ticket you want to use to call the requester.

  2. In the channel switcher menu of the message composer, click Call <number> or Call Enter a number.

  3. In the call console, confirm the number.

    You can then edit the outgoing number.

    The country code of the number will automatically change to match the number you’re calling.

  4. (Optional) Click Clear on Attaching to ticket <number> to create a new ticket for the call.

    This creates a new ticket.

  5. Click Call when you are ready to initiate the phone call.

    If you're using your own phone, Zendesk will call your number and then dial the recipient after you pick up.

Making an outbound call from a user profile

When you make a call from a user profile, the call details are added to a new ticket.

To make an outbound call from a user profile

  1. In Support, open a user's profile.

    See Viewing an end user's profile.

  2. Under Phone, click their number.

  3. Select Call this number.

    The call console opens with the user’s number.

  4. Click Call when you are ready to initiate the phone call.

    If you're using your own phone, Zendesk will call your number and then dial the recipient after you pick up.

articles.name: Announcing the end of support for AI agents - Essential and legacy functionality: Important dates and migration guidance
articles.title: Announcing the end of support for AI agents - Essential and legacy functionality: Important dates and migration guidance
articles.body:
This announcement applies to customers currently using AI agents - Essential or legacy AI agent functionality, such as bot builder and autoreplies with articles. This announcement does not apply to customers who previously purchased the AI agents - Advanced add-on.

We announced on March 30, 2026, that we are expanding access to our most advanced AI agent capabilities for all Zendesk customers. As part of this, we will be retiring the existing AI agents - Essential experience, default messaging responses, and legacy AI agent functionality (including bot builder, answers, and intents) in favor of advanced functionality included in the upgraded AI agent experience.

This transition ensures every customer can fully benefit from our more powerful AI agent experience, built to support more complex automation and a more consistent customer experience.

This announcement answers the following questions:

  • What’s changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What’s changing?

AI agents - Essential, default messaging responses, and legacy AI agent functionality are being retired. You will need to migrate to the upgraded AI agent experience, which includes agentic capabilities that were previously available only through the AI agents - Advanced add-on.  

Please carefully review the following key dates:

  • Beginning August 31, 2026, the legacy experience enters maintenance mode. Zendesk will stop technical development except for critical bug fixes and supporting breaking changes.
  • On December 10, 2026, AI agents - Essential and legacy functionality will officially reach end of service. Any AI agents left on this technology will no longer function.

It is recommended that all customers currently using AI agents - Essential, default messaging responses, or legacy AI agent functionality migrate to the new AI agent experience before August 31, 2026 to avoid disruption. Step-by-step migration documentation is linked in the What do I need to do? section of this announcement to support each configuration type.

Please note that as you begin benefiting from these changes, you may see changes in the number of automated resolutions (ARs) as you begin using upgraded capabilities. Because pricing is tied to outcomes, changes in the number of automated resolutions may impact your overall usage and associated costs. Your existing AR overage configuration will continue to be respected.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

We’re making this change to ensure all customers can begin benefiting from the full power of our latest AI innovations. After adopting the new AI agent experience, you’ll be able to automate more complex requests, resolve issues faster across channels, and scale support with expanded access to agentic capabilities, ultimately helping you deliver more resolutions with less effort.

What do I need to do?

You must migrate your essential or legacy AI agents and default messaging responses to the upgraded AI agent experience before December 10, 2026. However, we strongly recommend that you migrate even sooner—before August 31, 2026—to avoid any potential disruption.

We’ve created tailored migration guidance for each type of legacy functionality to ensure you have clear, step-by-step instructions based on your specific setup. This can be leveraged to begin migrating your experience today.

Looking for additional support? Zendesk Professional Services support is available to guide and assist your migration, helping ensure a smooth transition to the new AI agent experience with minimal disruption. Contact your account team to learn more. If you do not have a dedicated account team, please reach out to ssam@zendesk.com or start a conversation with our messaging widget.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Announcing free workforce activity tracking for Zendesk Suite plans and above
articles.title: Announcing free workforce activity tracking for Zendesk Suite plans and above
articles.body:
Starting June 24, a set of Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) capabilities will be available to Suite Professional plans and above at no additional cost.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

As part of Zendesk Suite, you’ll be able to opt in to a new Zendesk WFM experience that gives you better visibility into agent activity and daily average handle time (AHT). This information is available for 24 hours.

Agents can also see how much time they spend handling customer interactions.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

We’re making this change to help support teams gain better visibility into daily operations. By showing agent activity and AHT, managers can more easily understand workload trends, identify coaching opportunities, and improve planning. This also gives customers a simple way to get started with workforce visibility before exploring more advanced WFM capabilities.

What do I need to do?

This new experience will be rolled out on June 24, 2026 to Zendesk Suite plan customers. You can access our Zendesk WFM page from the Product menu to opt-in to this solution in case you want to try it out. To learn more, see Trying WFM for free using workforce activity tracking.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Trying WFM for free using workforce activity tracking
articles.title: Trying WFM for free using workforce activity tracking
articles.body:
Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) is an AI-driven operational tool that helps support teams optimize staffing by predicting ticket volume and automating agent schedules. By analyzing historical Zendesk data, it generates precise forecasts to ensure the right number of agents are available at the right times. Beyond scheduling, it provides managers with real-time visibility into agent activity and adherence, tracking whether team members are working on assigned tasks or taking breaks. Agents are also empowered to manage their own time through shift-swapping and paid time off (PTO) requests directly in the Zendesk interface.

What's my plan?
All Suites Professional, Enterprise, or Enterprise Plus

Summary: ◀▼

Workforce activity tracking helps you monitor agent activity in real time across email, messaging, and voice channels. It provides insights like average handle time and activity breakdowns, enabling better management of team performance. You can view, filter, and manage agent tasks without manual status updates. The feature is free to try and can be turned on or off anytime to evaluate workforce management capabilities.

Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) is an AI-driven operational tool that helps support teams optimize staffing by predicting ticket volume and automating agent schedules. By analyzing historical Zendesk data, it generates precise forecasts to ensure the right number of agents are available at the right times. Beyond scheduling, it provides managers with real-time visibility into agent activity and adherence, tracking whether team members are working on assigned tasks or taking breaks. Agents are also empowered to manage their own time through shift-swapping and paid time off (PTO) requests directly in the Zendesk interface.

Workforce activity tracking includes essential features, such as agent activity tracking and performance metrics, at no additional cost, so you can evaluate workforce management capabilities.

This article covers the following topics:

  • About workforce activity tracking
  • Getting started with workforce activity tracking
  • Using workforce activity tracking
  • Turning off workforce activity tracking

About workforce activity tracking

Workforce activity tracking lets you track agent activity in real time and view scheduled activities over time to help keep work aligned with planned workflows. It includes the core performance indicator average handle time (AHT), which helps measure efficiency.

Workforce activity tracking automatically maps tickets into workstreams and supports agent activity tracking across the following default channels:
  • Email
  • Messaging
  • Voice

This information is available for 24 hours. Workforce activity tracking doesn’t store historical data.

Specifically, admins can use the following features:

  • Real-time agent activity: Monitor what agents are working on as it happens. Get instant visibility into team activity with real-time dashboards and live insights.
  • Tracking by Group and Agent: View activity aggregated by group or drill down into individual agent performance.
  • Agent activity management: Add, edit, and delete agent activities. Note that adding, editing, and deleting agent activity tasks is restricted to the past 31 days.

Agents won't need to update their status manually because the system automatically handles activity tracking, which improves efficiency.

In Support, clicking the Workforce Management icon () in the top right corner opens the time tracker, including the ticket number and time spent on the task.

Getting started with workforce activity tracking

To get started viewing your agents’ activity on tickets translated into workstreams, turn on activity tracking.

To turn on workforce activity tracking

  1. Click the Zendesk product menu in the top bar, then select Workforce management.
  2. Click Try free version.

  3. Select Track agent activity.

    Tip: After activation, you can turn off workforce activity tracking at any time by deselecting agent activity tracking on the Account settings page in WFM.
  4. Click Save and start.

    You’re automatically directed to the WFM Agent activity page, where your agents’ activity starts synchronizing.

Using workforce activity tracking

After your agents’ activity is synced, you can start using the Agent activity page to do the following:

  • Monitor your agents’ activity and view what they’ve been working on. See Monitoring agent’s activity.
  • Filter the page by group, agent name or email address, date, or folder. See Viewing and filtering agent activity.
  • Open tickets related to an agent's activity.
  • View an agent's AHT and the activity breakdown by channel in the summary panel.

To view an agent’s activity using workforce activity tracking

  1. In Workforce management, click Monitoring in the sidebar, then select Agent activity.
  2. Click the name of the agent whose activity you want to view.

    The agent’s activity details expand.

  3. Hover over an entry to view additional information, such as the channels or task the agent is working on and the ticket ID.
  4. Click the agent activity task to open the associated ticket in Zendesk Support.
  5. Use the summary panel on the right to view the agent’s AHT and activity breakdown across email, messaging, and voice.

Turning off workforce activity tracking

After activation, you can turn off agent activity tracking in Account settings in WFM. However, doing so means synced activity is lost, agent activity tracking stops, and the Agent activity page is hidden.

You can still reactivate workforce activity tracking, but you’ll need to sync agent activity again.

To turn off agent activity

  1. In Workforce management, click Settings in the sidebar, then select Account settings.
  2. Deselect Track agent activity.

  3. Click Save.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-06-12
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-06-12
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Admin Center
  • AI agents
  • Copilot
  • Knowledge
  • App Marketplace
  • Products with no updates this week

Admin Center

New

  • There's a new setting for OAuth clients that allows admins to enroll their local OAuth clients to have their token get the default expiration applied. This means customers can take action themselves on their own schedule, ahead of the planned enforced expiration. There'll be a checkbox at the bottom of the Edit page for turning expiration on (Apps and integrations > APIs > OAuth clients > Edit OAuth client). Once turned on, it can't be turned off. For OAuth clients created after April 30, 2026, the checkbox will be disabled as these clients already have expirations set by default.

Fixed:

  • Audit log events for external OAuth clients now include the client name in the Activity column.

AI agents

  • Additional context for procedures. You can now add natural-language context to a generative procedure to give your AI agent behind-the-scenes guidance on how to execute it, such as steering tone, supplying domain knowledge, setting validation rules, or handling edge cases. Context can be added at the procedure, step, or block level, with broader context automatically applying to more specific levels. Available for messaging AI agents. See Adding context to a generative procedure for an AI agent.
  • New RAG agent. We've rolled out a new version of the RAG Agent's question-answering capability in the messaging channel, using a new architecture that dramatically improves safety and accuracy.
  • Voice: tags for tickets. Voice AI agents can now add tags to a ticket with a call. This is important because tags drive routing, reporting, and automations. This lets customers trigger those workflows directly from voice conversations. Just enter one or more tags in the action's tags field, and they'll be applied to the ticket.

Copilot

New:

  • Starting June 11, 2026, we're launching updates for Copilot intelligent triage that lower the effort to get started, improve over time, and expand across Zendesk. From guided onboarding to smarter topic suggestions, this release builds on our recent announcement, moving from "intents" to "topics," and continues our core vision: giving your team the foundation to automate more tickets, power smarter workflows, and scale your support operations with confidence. See Intelligent triage that's easier to start, scale, and manage.

Knowledge

New:

  • Introduction of Google Drive Connector for External Knowledge Sources. Starting June 11, 2026, Knowledge admins can connect shared Google Drives to Zendesk as an external knowledge source. Content will automatically sync every 24 hours (or manually on demand), allowing organizations to leverage their existing Google Docs and Sheets across multiple Zendesk knowledge experiences without manual content duplication. See Connecting Google Drive as an external knowledge source.
  • Introduction of Zendesk Connector for external content. Starting June 9, 2026, customers can connect a second Zendesk account or sandbox to their primary account. All published Help Center articles from the connected brand will be ingested as external content in the destination account. Rollout will begin on June 9 and will be completed on June 19, 2026. See Connecting Zendesk as an external knowledge source.
  • Editable Procedures. Starting 11 June 2026, customers can edit a generative procedure directly in the visual canvas instead of regenerating it. Select any block to update its prompts, messages, conditions, actions, or integrations, switch block types (for example, convert a Link to block into an Escalate to block), and reset or restore changes at any time. Edited blocks are highlighted so you can see what changed. You can edit existing blocks, but cannot yet add, reorder, or delete them. Available across all channels. See Managing generative procedures for AI agents.

App Marketplace

New:
  • PilotFlow PRO (Support) (paid)
    • PilotFlow PRO is a powerful automation engine built for Zendesk teams that require precision, scalability, and full control over their support workflows. Go beyond the limitations of native triggers by creating advanced rule systems that handle complex scenarios in real time. Automatically assign tickets, apply tags, manage followers, and orchestrate workflows using multi-layered conditional logic. 
  • Flexivity AI (Support)
    • Flexivity AI is an AI agent assist for Zendesk that helps your team triage, draft, summarize, and learn from every ticket. Trained on your team's history, with transparent confidence scores and full agent control over every response. Auto-classify incoming tickets using categories learned from your team's ticket history, suggest responses based on similar past tickets and relevant knowledge base articles, with reference links — no manual searching required, and surface key facts, decisions, and current sentiment so any agent can pick up where another left off.
  • Anthem (Theme)
    • Anthem is a dark, rhythmic Zendesk Help Center theme designed for brands that live in the music, audio, and streaming space. A hot-pink-to-gold gradient system pulses across a deep violet base, with waveform-inspired touches, rounded cards, and glow-on-hover interactions that feel like album art rather than a ticket system. Sora headings bring geometric boldness; inter-body text stays out of the way and lets the energy do the talking.
  • Civic (Theme)
    • Civic is an accessible-first Zendesk Help Center theme designed for government agencies, public services, and civic technology organizations. Built in the spirit of the U.S. Web Design System (USWDS), it uses the USWDS blue banner bar, Public Sans typography (the official USWDS typeface), and high-contrast layouts to ensure every constituent can find what they need, regardless of device or ability. Clarity and trust are not optional here, and the design reflects that.
  • Smao (Support)
    • Smao is an AI-powered phone assistant that handles incoming calls and lets you define automated workflows based on call events. Smao turns every call into a structured, actionable process. The smao-Zendesk integration allows you to create Zendesk tickets directly from within smao workflows. When a caller triggers a defined workflow - for example, requesting support - Smao can automatically create a fully populated ticket in Zendesk, including ticket type, priority, tags, and the associated contact.

Products with no updates this week

  • Support
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • Voice
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Announcing team visibility settings for time off reasons
articles.title: Announcing team visibility settings for time off reasons
articles.body:
Zendesk WFM is introducing a new team visibility setting for time off reasons, giving managers and admins greater control over which teams can see and use specific time off reasons. Instead of every agent on an account having access to all time off reasons, you can now tailor leave options to reflect your teams' specific locations, perks, and policies.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Time off reasons will now include a teams setting, allowing managers to control which teams can see and use each reason. 

When creating or editing a time off reason, managers can select one or more teams from a new Teams field, and agents will only see the reasons assigned to their team when submitting a time off request. The Time off reasons page will reflect this with a new Teams column, and bulk actions will allow managers to add or remove team assignments across multiple reasons at once.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Currently, all time off reasons are visible to every agent on a given account, which isn’t granular enough for most customers.

It’s common to have specific reasons that only some teams can use, such as leave types tied to geographical location, perks granted to specialist teams, or manager-only leave types that agents can’t request themselves but that managers and admins can still manually assign in the Schedule.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. When this feature rolls out, all existing teams will automatically be added to all existing time off reasons, ensuring no disruption to your current setup. After the migration, we encourage you to review your Time off reasons and adjust the team assignments as needed to make sure everything is configured to your requirements. See Creating and managing time off reasons for agents.
